document_metadata.title: Vibrational Spectrum of Sodium Azide Crystals
document_metadata.author: Unknown Author
document_metadata.page_source: Bryant - 1964 - Vibrational Spectrum of Sodium Azide Single Crystals 2-06

sample_description:
- **Document Title, Author, Year, and Publication Details**: 
  - Title: Vibrational Spectrum of Sodium Azide Crystals
  - Authors and specific publication details are not visible on this page.

- **Sample Description and Experimental Conditions**: 
  - The sample is sodium azide (NaN₃) single crystals.
  - Spectra were measured at different temperatures: 298°K and 90°K.
  - Crystal thicknesses used in experiments: 0.1 mm, 0.5 mm, and 0.8 mm.

- **Spectroscopic Technique Used**: 
  - Infrared sp
overall_findings:
unpolarized spectra were analyzed.
  - Raman spectra were obtained using a cross polarizer.

- **Overall Findings or Conclusions from the Page**: 
  - The Raman spectrum of NaN₃ shows three distinct depolarized bands.
  - The line at 122 cm⁻¹ corresponds to the rotary lattice mode.
  - The strong line at 1358 cm⁻¹ is identified as the symmetric stretching mode.
  - Tentative assignments of vibrational modes are made based on experimental data, although numerical calculations were not performed.
